title: IBM Research Loses Top Quantum Scientist to European Startup
body:
IBM Research confirmed Friday that Dr. Elena Vasquez, one of its most prominent quantum hardware researchers, has resigned to take the role of Chief Technology Officer at Volta Systems, the well-funded German startup.

Vasquez spent eleven years at IBM's Yorktown Heights lab, where she led the team responsible for the Heron processor architecture. Her departure is a blow to IBM's quantum roadmap at a time when competition from Google and domestic European players has intensified.

In a brief statement, IBM said it "wishes Elena well" and would announce a successor "in due course." IBM declined to say whether a retention offer was made.

Volta confirmed the hire in a separate press release, describing Vasquez as "the ideal leader" for its hardware engineering organization. The appointment is effective March 1. Volta's CEO, Rolf Andersen, said the company's newly completed Series C gave it the resources to attract world-class talent it previously could not compete for.

Vasquez did not respond to requests for comment before publication.
